metadata.title: Paediatric addendum to the note for guidance on the clinical investigation on medicinal products in the treatment of hypertension

rules.rule_text: This addendum must be read in conjunction with the adult Guideline on Clinical Investigation of Medicinal Products in the Treatment of Hypertension (EMA/238/1995/Rev. 3, 18 November 2010).
rules.verbatim_quote: This is an addendum to the Guideline on Clinical Investigation of Medicinal Products in the Treatment of Hypertension (EMA/238/1995/Rev. 3, 18 November 2010). It is not meant as a guidance document on its own but rather highlights differences from adult patients with arterial hypertension and points out paediatric-specific aspects.

rules.rule_text: Hypertension in children and adolescents is defined as systolic BP and/or diastolic BP that is, on repeated measurement, at or above the 95th percentile.
rules.verbatim_quote: Hypertension in children and adolescents is defined as systolic BP (SBP) and/or diastolic BP (DBP) that is, on repeated measurement, at or above the 95th percentile.
rules.exceptions: BP between the 90th and 95th percentile is designated 'high normal'.

rules.rule_text: Guidance applies to design of clinical studies for evaluation of antihypertensive drugs in children of all age groups (0-18 years), with more attention to younger patients with mostly secondary forms of hypertension.
rules.verbatim_quote: Guidance is provided on the design of clinical studies considered to be of relevance for the evaluation of antihypertensive drugs in children of all age groups (0-18 years). More attention is devoted to the younger patients with mostly secondary forms of hypertension.
rules.exceptions: Fixed dose combinations and products for immediate BP control are not covered.

rules.rule_text: Beneficial effects of antihypertensive treatment in children must be extrapolated from evidence in adults. Post-authorisation long-term follow-up and observational research are encouraged.
rules.verbatim_quote: the beneficial effects of antihypertensive treatment in children have to be extrapolated from evidence obtained in adults. ... Post-authorisation long-term follow up and observational research are encouraged to better understand the clinical correlation of antihypertensive treatment in childhood.
rules.exceptions: Extrapolation is relatively reliable for essential hypertension, but effects in secondary hypertension are difficult to relate to the adult population.

rules.rule_text: Establishing an effect on morbidity and mortality endpoints is not required in paediatric licensing trials of antihypertensive medicinal products.
rules.verbatim_quote: Establishing an effect on morbidity and mortality endpoints is not required in paediatric licensing trials of antihypertensive medicinal products.

rules.rule_text: Reduction in BP is accepted as a valid surrogate endpoint in paediatric hypertension trials.
rules.verbatim_quote: Reduction in BP is accepted as a valid surrogate endpoint in order to assess whether the goal to prevent morbidity and mortality associated with high BP can be achieved by an antihypertensive agent.

rules.rule_text: The effect on the kidney should be regularly monitored in paediatric clinical trials of hypertension.
rules.verbatim_quote: The effect on the kidney should be regularly monitored in the paediatric clinical trials of hypertension.

rules.rule_text: Assessment of presence and progression of other types of organ damage is advisable in longer-term studies to clarify the relationship between BP reduction and organ protection.
rules.verbatim_quote: Assessment of presence and progression of other types of organ damage is advisable in longer-term studies in children to clarify the relationship between the BP reduction and organ protection.

rules.rule_text: The preferred method of BP measurement is auscultation by office/clinic measurements. Correct measurement requires a cuff appropriate to the size of the child's upper arm.
rules.verbatim_quote: The preferred method of BP measurement is auscultation by office/clinic measurements and correct measurement requires a cuff that is appropriate to the size of the child's upper arm.

rules.rule_text: BP lowering effects should be documented as the pre-/post-treatment reduction of BP. Timing of measurement with once daily dosing should be at 24-hours post-dose (trough).
rules.verbatim_quote: The timing of measurement in most paediatric studies with once daily dosing has been at 24-hours post-dose ('trough'). Similarly to the adult studies, the BP lowering effects of anti-hypertensive therapy should be documented as the pre-/post-treatment reduction of BP.

rules.rule_text: Paediatric BP control may be defined as BP below the 95th age-, sex- and height-specific percentile, though use of the 90th percentile has also been advocated. The cut-off used must be justified.
rules.verbatim_quote: paediatric BP control may be defined as a BP below the 95th age-, sex- and height-specific percentiles, but it has also been advocated to use a BP below the 90th percentile. The cut-off used should be justified.
rules.exceptions: Lower BP targets may be used in renal and diabetic disease in children when justified based on relevant paediatric data.

rules.rule_text: Wider use of ABPM (ambulatory blood pressure monitoring) in paediatric clinical trials is encouraged where appropriate and feasible.
rules.verbatim_quote: In spite of the limited availability of normative data it may still be possible to use these methods to measure within- subject treatment effects. Their wider use in clinical trials is thus encouraged where appropriate and feasible.
rules.exceptions: Practical challenges exist in younger patients.

rules.rule_text: Renal damage must be assessed using GFR (calculated by Schwartz formula or other properly evaluated equations) and albuminuria. Multiple GFR estimations over time should be obtained where feasible.
rules.verbatim_quote: Renal insufficiency has usually been classified according to the glomerular filtration rate (GFR) calculated by the Schwartz formula. ... Where feasible, multiple estimations of GFR over time should be obtained to better estimate renal disease progression.

rules.rule_text: Both GFR and albuminuria should be measured to characterise the long-term renal effect of antihypertensive therapy in children.
rules.verbatim_quote: both GFR and albuminuria should be measured to better characterise the effect long-term.

rules.rule_text: Left ventricular measures should be standardized to height to minimize the effect of changes in body size during childhood.
rules.verbatim_quote: Left ventricular measures should be standardized to height to minimize the effect of changes in body size during childhood.

rules.rule_text: If hypertension is detected by oscillometric methods, it must be confirmed by the auscultatory method.
rules.verbatim_quote: if hypertension is detected by the oscillometric methods, it must be confirmed by the auscultatory method.

rules.rule_text: Organ damage evaluation should include kidney, heart, great vessels, central nervous system and retina where possible.
rules.verbatim_quote: Organ damage evaluation should include kidney, heart, great vessels, central nervous system and retina where possible.

rules.rule_text: All age groups should be adequately represented to allow right dosing and safe use. A step-wise approach for involving the youngest age groups is required after safety is established in older patients.
rules.verbatim_quote: All age groups should be adequately represented to allow right dosing and safe use. It may be necessary to use step-wise approach in involving the youngest age groups after the safety has been established in the older patients.

rules.rule_text: For products with new mechanism of action and in younger age groups, dedicated dose-ranging and safety studies are always necessary; extrapolation from adult studies or same-class agents should be avoided.
rules.verbatim_quote: This is not the case for products with new mechanism of action and in younger age groups where dedicated dose-ranging and safety studies are always necessary.
rules.exceptions: Data on efficacy and safety in treating essential hypertension in adolescents may under certain circumstances be extrapolated from adult studies or same-class agents already studied.

rules.rule_text: Stratification of randomization according to aetiology or patient characteristics (e.g., CKD/non-CKD) needs to be discussed.
rules.verbatim_quote: Stratification of randomization according to the aetiology or patient characteristics needs to be discussed, e.g. CKD/ non-CKD patients.

rules.rule_text: PK data for all relevant paediatric age groups must be provided. Measures to minimise pain and distress due to blood sampling must be foreseen and described (e.g., less invasive sampling, sparse sampling, population PK).
rules.verbatim_quote: PK data for all relevant paediatric age groups should be provided. ... Measures to minimise pain and distress due to blood sampling in studies need to be foreseen and described (including study methods e.g. less invasive sampling techniques, sparse sampling and population PK).

rules.rule_text: For children 1 to <6 years, a formulation allowing adequate dosing flexibility is mandatory to assure reliable administration and accurate weight-adjusted dosing. A commercial paediatric formulation must be developed in time for paediatric studies.
rules.verbatim_quote: For children 1 to < 6 years of age, a formulation that allows adequate dosing flexibility is a must to assure reliable administration and accurate weight-adjusted dosing. All efforts must be made to develop a commercial paediatric formulation in time to use this formulation during paediatric studies.

rules.rule_text: Double-blind randomized studies are requested to establish effective therapeutic doses. Two accepted designs are: 1) randomised parallel study with >=2 dose arms followed by randomised withdrawal to placebo, or 2) placebo arm and >=2 dose arms.
rules.verbatim_quote: Double-blind randomized studies are requested to establish effective therapeutic doses. ... Two designs used most often have been a randomised, double-blind parallel study with 1) >=2 arms receiving doses of the test drug followed by a randomised withdrawal to placebo and 2) placebo arm and >=2 arms receiving different doses of the test drug.
rules.exceptions: Use of a parallel placebo group in very young and more seriously affected patients may not be feasible.

rules.rule_text: Dosing must be weight-adjusted (per kg). The dose range should provide exposure from slightly lower than the lowest approved adult dose to somewhat higher than the highest approved adult dose.
rules.verbatim_quote: The dosing regimen needs to ensure little or no overlap between the dose categories tested, preferably by using individual subject weight adjusted (per kg) dosing. ... Doses providing exposure from slightly lower than the lowest approved adult dose up to somewhat higher than the highest approved dose in adults (unless restricted by safety concerns) could be considered.

rules.rule_text: The primary endpoint for dose-finding studies should be the change from baseline in Mean Sitting Diastolic Blood Pressure (MSDBP) or Mean Sitting Systolic Blood Pressure (MSSBP). If one is chosen as primary, the other serves as secondary. BP response and control rates must also be included as endpoints.
rules.verbatim_quote: The primary endpoint for the dose-finding studies should be the change from baseline Mean Sitting Diastolic Blood Pressure (MSDBP) or Mean Sitting Systolic Blood Pressure (MSSBP) ... If MSDBP is chosen as a primary endpoint, the MSSBP will serve as a secondary and vice versa. BP response and control rates should also be included as endpoints.

rules.rule_text: The trial program is expected to have a total of no less than 300 paediatric patients for safety reasons to identify adverse reactions occurring with a 1% frequency.
rules.verbatim_quote: The trial program is expected to have a total of no less than 300 paediatric patients for safety reasons to identify adverse reactions occurring with a 1% frequency.

rules.rule_text: At least 12-month extension studies are necessary to allow investigation of long-term safety in terms of growth (head circumference, weight and height) and development, including neurocognitive development.
rules.verbatim_quote: At least 12-month extension studies are necessary to allow investigation of long-term safety in terms of growth (head circumference, weight and height) and development, including neurocognitive development.
rules.exceptions: Longer follow-up may be appropriate for assessment of end-organ damage or for new drug classes.

rules.rule_text: Younger age groups (infants, children under 6 years) must be adequately represented and may need to be followed up longer (e.g., 24 months). Safety concerns in infants may need to be addressed by step-wise recruitment or interim safety data analysis before inclusion of youngest patients.
rules.verbatim_quote: Younger age groups (infants, children under 6 years of age) have to be adequately represented and may need to be followed up longer (e.g. 24 months). ... Specific safety concerns during the studies in infants may need to be addressed by step-wise recruitment to the trials (interim safety data analysis before the inclusion of the youngest patients) or justified cut-off age.
